title: Quality Guide for Reviewers
purpose: Explains how to correctly fill out the Quality Review Form for accurate, fair, and automated scoring. Covers deduction logic, flag types, and approved subcategories.

regular_comment.when_to_use: Issue appears for the first time and is not flaggable. Add in comments section only.

yellow_flag.when_to_use: Issue is flaggable and it's the first occurrence. Write in both comments AND yellow flag section.
yellow_flag.example: Yellow Flag - Dress Code: The instructor was wearing pajamas during the session. Kindly adhere to our dress code policy. [Full session]

red_flag.when_to_use: Repeated issue after a yellow flag. Reuse the EXACT SAME comment wording to trigger system deduction tracking.
red_flag.example: Red Flag - Camera Quality: The instructor turned off the camera during the session. Kindly note that the camera should remain on throughout the entire session. [00:30:50 - 01:30:20]

repeated_red_flag.when_to_use: Issue persists after a red flag. Again reuse exact same wording.

comment_format.format: [Category Letter] - [Subcategory Name]: [Clear, professional description of the issue]
comment_format.example: T - Project Implementation & Activities: Students didn't follow the planned activity, and the tutor didn't guide them back on track.

common_mistakes: Using category names that are not on the approved list
Rewording repeated issues — this breaks the deduction tracking system
Forgetting to check the tutor's history before reviewing
Mixing red/yellow flags or using both sections for the same issue
Tutor ID must be written as T-XXXX (capital T) for system deduction to work
Forgetting to check whether the tutor was informed about the flag before the session
Not checking the Mentorship session before reviewing

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content:
This guide explains how to correctly fill out the Quality Review Form to ensure accurate, fair,
and automated scoring. It also outlines how to handle issues, how deductions are applied, and
provides a full list of approved subcategories for writing structured comments and flags also on
how to use the sheet.

sections.heading: Quality Sheet
sections.content: DEMO

sections.heading: 📑 The Quality Check tab
sections.content:
helps track all assigned reviews and their completion status. Key columns include:
● Columns A- C: Tutor data (ID, name, assigned mentor).
● Column D: The assigned reviewer is responsible for conducting the session
review.
● Column E: Role selection (Mentor/Coordinator).
● Column F: Group ID of the session being reviewed.
● Column G: to check after submitting the form so it will be counted as done

sections.heading: Group ID Selection Rule
sections.content:
● If a tutor is reviewed in Weeks 3 & 4, the next reviewer in Weeks 5 & 6 must select a
different Group ID than the first reviewer.
● If a tutor is reviewed again in Weeks 7 & 8, the reviewer must choose a different Group
ID than both previous reviewers.
● Example:
○ Week 2: Mohamed Elsayed reviews Tutor T-1234 under Group ID A.
○ Week 3: Nouran (Reviewer 2) must select Group ID B (different from A).
○ Week 4: If another reviewer (Reviewer 3) is assigned, they must choose Group
ID C (different from A and B).

sections.heading: 📑 The Counts tab:
sections.content:
To track your assigned quality review sessions. It includes:
● The total number of sessions assigned to you
● Your progress
● Your target completion count
● A leaderboard showing reviewers who finish on time

sections.heading: 📝 Edit Hub Tab:
sections.content:
Use this tab if you submitted the form with a mistake or need to make modifications.
For example, suppose you forgot to add a negative comment. In that case, you can replace the
entire "Negative Comments" section with the updated version, along with the correct data
related to that session.
🛑 Important:
Once a form is submitted without a flag, you cannot edit the response to add one later, even
after assurance.
However, you can edit the response sheet to change an existing flag from
Red → Yellow
Red →  None
Yellow →  Red
Yellow → None
Both →  Red
Both→ Yellow
Both→ None

sections.heading: 📑Flag tab
sections.content:
● Mentors must check the Red Flags tab and inform their tutors about any flags they
have received ASAP.
● You will find Col Message, which contains the message so you can send it directly to the
tutor and contains a form so the tutor can fill it out if they think the flag was issued
incorrectly.
● Once the tutor has been informed, check the corresponding box next to their name to
confirm acknowledgment.

sections.heading: How to Use the Review Form:
sections.content:
Check the Tutor’s History Before Reviewing:
● Always check the previous reviews for the same tutor.
● If an issue has already been flagged or commented on before, and it repeats, this
affects how you enter the issue and how many points are deducted

sections.heading: Check the Session Issue Form
sections.content:
Before selecting a comment or writing a flag, you must check the Session Issues Form for the
specific session being reviewed.
✅ Example of a Valid Justification (No Flag):
● Issue: The Tutor joined the session late
● Justification: “The dashboard was down.”
● ✅ Confirmed downtime → No flag should be added
❌ Example of an Invalid Justification (Flag Required):
● Issue: The Tutor joined the session late
● Justification: “I overslept.”
● ❌ Unacceptable reason → Flag should be added

sections.heading: Check if the Tutor Was Informed About the Previous Flag
sections.content:
Before changing a yellow flag to red, make sure the tutor has already been informed about
the previous flag.
None
● ❌ If the tutor was not informed, do not change the flag to red. You may still issue a

sections.heading: yellow flag.
sections.content:
● ✅ If the tutor was informed before the session you are reviewing, and the issue is
repeated, change the flag from yellow to red.
This ensures fair treatment and accurate deductions.

sections.heading: Regular Comments:
sections.content:
● Use when the issue appears for the first time and is not flaggable. Add in the comments
section only.

sections.heading: Yellow Flag:
sections.content: ● Use when the issue is flaggable, but it's the first occurrence. Write the issue in both the

sections.heading: comments and the yellow flag section.
sections.content:
✅ Example:
Comment choosen
S - Dress Code: Kindly ensure a professional appearance,
including a well-groomed presentation and adherence to
semi-formal/formal attire, during sessions.

sections.heading: Yellow Flag sentence:
sections.content: ✅ Example:

sections.heading: Yellow Flag - Dress Code: The instructor was wearing pajamas
sections.content:
during the session. Kindly adhere to our dress code policy. [Full
session]
None
None

sections.heading: Red Flag:
sections.content: ● Now it's a repeated issue.

sections.heading: ● Write it as a red flag in the Red Flag section.
sections.content:
● Use the same comment that was used previously to ensure the system applies double
deductions.
○ (e.g, “S - Camera Quality: Please note that it is mandatory to keep the webcam
activated throughout the entire session.”)
✅ Example:

sections.heading: Red Flag - Camera Quality: The instructor turned off the camera
sections.content:
during the session. Kindly note that the camera should remain on
throughout the entire session to ensure effective communication.
[00:30:50 - 01:30:20]

sections.heading: Repeated Red Flag:
sections.content:
● Again, reuse the same comment from the comment session used before to trigger an
even higher deduction.

sections.heading: Write Structured Comments (in Comments Section)
sections.content:
For each issue, write your comment in this format:
[Category Letter] - [Subcategory Name]: [Clear, professional
description of the issue]
✅ Example:
None
None
T - Project Implementation & Activities: Students didn’t follow
the planned activity, and the tutor didn’t guide them back on
track.
🛑 Important:

sections.heading: ● Only use the approved subcategories listed below.
sections.content:
● If the issue isn’t listed in the form:
1. First, search for it in the DEMI Quality Comment Bank.
2. If it’s not found, follow this format:
[Category Letter] - [Subcategory Name]: [Positive aspect] +
[Identified issue] + [Suggestion for improvement or how it
affected the session]

sections.heading: 🧮 How Deductions Work (Automatically by Script)
sections.content:
Issue Type
Deduction Logic
📝 First-time comment (non-flag issue)
-1 point from score

sections.heading: 🟨 First-time Yellow Flag
sections.content: -1 point from score

sections.heading: -2 from repeated comment + -1 red flag = -3 total
sections.content:
🧠 Why it matters: The script automatically checks if the same comment has been added
before. If so, it applies to increasing deductions.
✅ Using the same wording allows the system to track and score it correctly.
None
None
None

sections.heading: 🧾 Example Review Entry
sections.content:
Comments Section:
T - Class Management: The session lacked clear guidance, causing
students to work in different directions.
S - Homework: Homework was not reviewed or followed up on during
the session.

sections.heading: Red Flag - Dress Code: The tutor wore a hoodie and appeared
sections.content: unprofessional. [Full session]

sections.heading: ✅ Approved Subcategories (for Copy-Paste Use)
sections.content:
Paste these exactly in your comments or flag entries:
Camera Quality
Microphone Quality
Environment
Internet Quality
Dress Code
Voice Tone & Clarity
Language Used
Session Initiation & Closure
Friendliness
Session study
Project software & slides
Knowledge About Subject
Slides and project completion
Tools used and Methodology
Homework
Class Management
Student Engagement
All Student’s Involvement
Session Synchronization
Project Implementation & Activities

sections.heading: ⚠️ Common Mistakes to Avoid
sections.content:
● ❌ Using category names that are not on the list
● ❌ Writing vague or unstructured comments
● ❌ Rewording repeated issues — this breaks the deduction tracking

sections.heading: ● ❌ Forgetting to check the tutor's history
sections.content:
● ❌ Mixing red/yellow flags or using both sections at the same time for the same issue
● ❌ Tutor ID must be written in the format T-XXXX (with a capital "T") to ensure the
deduction is properly recorded by the system.
● ❌ Forgetting to check whether the tutor had already been informed about the flag
before the session you are reviewing.
● ❌ Not checking the Mentorship session before reviewing a session
● ❌Checked a session as reviewed, while it’s not submitted

sections.heading: 📌 Final Notes
sections.content:
● Always keep comments objective, clear, and professional.
● Use complete sentences and explain both what happened and why it matters.
If you’re unsure whether an issue counts as a yellow/red flag, consult your mentor or the DEMI
Quality Team.
